key_themes: Robust revenue growth and raised FY26 guidance amid strong defense demand
Analyst upgrades and buy ratings with significant upside targets
Mixed Q4 earnings with EPS miss but overall profitability improvements
High valuation concerns and post-earnings stock volatility
summary: Karman Holdings (KRMN) showed strong Q4 2025 revenue growth of 47% YoY, beating estimates slightly, while raising FY26 guidance to $715M-$730M on surging missile defense demand, supported by a $1B+ backlog and multiple analyst upgrades to buy with targets up to $135. However, an EPS miss on the reported $0.11 (consensus met on adjusted but not GAAP) led to a 14% stock drop post-earnings, highlighting ongoing concerns over free cash flow generation and elevated valuations at 123x forward earnings. The biggest risk is sector rotation away from defense amid broader market pressures and potential delays in contract execution.
